data.title: GSR says its OTC trading desk remains extremely active and able to handle client requests
data.description: GSR stated in a post on the X platform that its over-the-counter (OTC) trading platform remains extremely active and able to handle client requests. During periods of extreme volatility, GSR made appropriate adjustments to quotes and continued to provide liquidity for most tokens.

data.title: DeFi celebrates stress test wins as market liquidations reach record levels
data.description:
Decentralized finance (DeFi) leaders are hailing the resilience of blockchain-based trading and lending protocols after one of the largest market sell-offs of the year, triggered by renewed trade tensions between the United States and China. The turbulence came after President Donald Trump announced that starting November 1, he will impose new 100% tariffs on Chinese […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/defi-stress-test-market-liquidations-record/

data.title: Over 6,300 wallets on Hyperliquid lost over $1.23 billion during the market downturn
data.description: According to Lookonchain, over 1,000 wallets on Hyperliquid were completely liquidated during the market downturn. This left over 6,300 wallets in the red, collectively holding over $1.23 billion. Of these, 205 wallets lost over $1 million, and over 1,070 lost over $100,000.

data.title: The eighth-largest account on the Hyperliquid platform was completely liquidated, with losses potentially reaching $18-19 million.
data.description: According to MLM monitoring, the account "Cyantarb", which ranked eighth in trading volume on Hyperliquid, was fully liquidated early this morning (about 16 hours ago), with losses potentially reaching $18 million to $19 million. This liquidation resulted in a loss of approximately $100 million in its nominal position, making it one of the accounts with the largest liquidation amounts on the Hyperliquid platform in the past 24 hours.

data.title: Tether and Circle issue $1.75 billion in stablecoins after market drop
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, after the market fell, Tether and Circle issued a total of US$1.75 billion in stablecoins.

data.title: Cango announced that its BTC holdings have exceeded 6,000
data.description: Bitcoin mining company Cango stated in a post on the X platform that as of October 11, 2025, Cango holds a total of 6,004.64 BTC.

data.title: Crypto.com CEO calls on regulators to investigate exchanges involved in mass liquidations
data.description: Crypto.com CEO Kris posted on the X platform, urging regulators to investigate exchanges that experienced large-scale liquidations in the past 24 hours and conduct a thorough review of the fairness of their practices. This review should include: whether trading system downtime or delays prevented users from trading; whether all trades were priced in line with the index; the effectiveness of transaction monitoring and anti-money laundering (AML) procedures; and whether internal trading teams were effectively segregated. He noted that $20 billion in liquidations harmed a significant number of users, and that regulators' responsibility was to protect consumers and ensure market integrity.

data.title: DWF Labs Managing Partner: The market decline was triggered by the tariff announcement and leveraged liquidation
data.description: Andrei Grachev, managing partner and co-founder of DWF Labs, wrote on the X platform that the market decline was not caused by fundamental factors such as the FTX crash, but rather by the tariff announcement and subsequent leveraged liquidations. Liquidity has been depleted, but Bitcoin and strong projects should recover quickly.

data.title: Tom Lee: Today's decline is a good time to buy
data.description: Tom Lee commented on today's market flash crash: "The decline was expected. We have already seen a 36% increase since the April low. Today's VIX index rose by 29% at one point, the 51st largest single-day fluctuation in history, and is in the top 1% of extreme performance. Today's decline is a good shakeout. What I want to tell you is that the market is indeed a bit nervous, but unless there are real structural changes, this pullback is a good buying opportunity. I can't say that the market has bottomed out today, but we know that under the current circumstances, the earnings performance in the next week or even month will be good. If someone asks me how the market will perform in a week, I would answer that the probability of an increase is quite high."

data.title: Bitfarms converts $300 million in debt financing into project financing for Pennsylvania data center construction
data.description: Bitcoin News published an article on the X platform stating that Canadian Bitcoin mining company Bitfarms has converted its $300 million debt financing with Macquarie into project-specific financing to fund the construction of the first phase of its Panther Creek data center in Pennsylvania. The data center is expected to be powered by the end of 2026.

data.title: ZEC briefly broke through 290 USDT, with a 24-hour increase of 18.31%.
data.description: Market data shows that ZEC briefly broke through 290 USDT and is currently trading at 279.7 USDT, with a 24-hour increase of 18.31%.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 1,130 USDT with a Narrowed 10.21% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Oct 11, 2025, 13:46 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 1,130 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 1,130.180054 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 10.21% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Coinbase to test $5,000 transaction limit for all customers
data.description: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong announced on the X platform that Coinbase will test a $5,000 trading limit for all customers starting October 14. Furthermore, the company plans to deploy tiered trading limits of up to $10,000 (and potentially $100,000) for low-risk users by the middle of next year.

data.title: Ethena Labs: USDe still has approximately $66 million in overcollateralization
data.description: Ethena Labs stated in a post on the X platform that, in response to community requests and following recent market events, it has provided a proof of reserves outside of its regular schedule. Independent third parties, including Chaos Labs, Chainlink, Llama Risk, and Harris & Trotter, have confirmed that USDe remains approximately $66 million overcollateralized.

data.title: Ukrainian crypto blogger Kudo died in Kyiv, police initially ruled it a suicide
data.description: According to RBC-Ukraine, 32-year-old Ukrainian cryptocurrency blogger Konstantin Ganich, nicknamed Kudo, has been found dead in Kyiv. Police found his body in a car with a gunshot wound to the head, next to a weapon registered to him. The initial diagnosis is suicide. Kudo was a well-known cryptocurrency blogger and CEO of Cryptology, a financial and trading consulting and education company. In a recent interview, Kudo stated that his partner had stolen his funds and disappeared. He also expressed depression due to financial difficulties to family and friends, and left a suicide note.

data.title: Stablecoin Issuance Surges Amid Market Downturn
data.description: According to TechFlow, on October 11, blockchain analysis platform Lookonchain reported that Tether and Circle collectively issued $1.75 billion in stablecoins following a market decline. This issuance reflects the ongoing demand for stablecoins as investors seek refuge from market volatility. The move by these centralized exchange (CEX) operators highlights the role of stablecoins in providing liquidity and stability in the cryptocurrency market.
